title: Load sharing bearing
abstract: The present invention relates to a load sharing component (200) for mounting between an auxiliary line component and a tubular, the load sharing component comprising a resilient bearing member (201). The load sharing component may be used with a riser or landing string and the resilient bearing member can in use be configured to afford limited resilient deformation in an axial direction of the tubular and to resist deformation in a radial direction of the tubular.
claims: Claims Claims 1. A load sharing component for mounting between an auxiliary line component and a tubular, the load sharing component comprising a resilient bearing member. 2. The load sharing component of claim 1 , wherein the tubular is a riser or landing string. 3. The load sharing component of claim 1 or 2, wherein the resilient bearing member in use is configured to afford limited resilient deformation in an axial direction of the tubular and to resist deformation in a radial direction of the tubular. 4. The load sharing component of any preceding claim, wherein the load sharing component is configured for mounting at the interface between an auxiliary load sharing member of an auxiliary line component, selected from one or more of a rod, choke or kill line, and a flange of a tubular section. 5. The load sharing component of any preceding claim, wherein the resilient bearing member is formed of at least one rigid element and at least one elastomeric element. 6. The load sharing component of claim 5, wherein the resilient bearing member is formed of a plurality of rigid elements separated along the axial direction by elastomeric material of the at least one elastomeric element. 7. The load sharing component of claim 6, comprising a stack of alternating rigid element shims and elastomeric shims bonded together. 8. The load sharing component of any one of claims 6 or 7, wherein the plurality of rigid elements are arranged to lie normally relative to the tubular axis. 9. The load sharing component of any one of claims 6 to 8, wherein the resilient bearing member has an annular form. 10. The load sharing component of claims 7, wherein the rigid element and elastomeric shims have a common inner and outer diameter. 11. The load sharing component of any preceding claim, wherein a dedicated resilient bearing member is provided to each auxiliary line component. 12. The load sharing component of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the resilient bearing member is shared between a number of auxiliary line components. 13. The load sharing component of any preceding claim, further comprising a pre-tensioning element. 14. The load sharing component of claim 13, wherein the pre-tensioning element is disposed between the resilient bearing member and the auxiliary line component. 15. The load sharing component of any one of claims 13 or 14, wherein the pre tensioning element is positioned directly adjacent to the resilient bearing member. 16. The load sharing component of claims 13 to 15, wherein the pre-tensioning element comprises an elastomeric member. 17. The load sharing component of claims 13 to 16, wherein the elastomeric member of the pre-tensioning element has a lower axial stiffness than that of the resilient bearing member. 18. The load sharing component of claim 16 or 17 wherein the elastomeric member is arranged between first and second clamp members of the pre-tensioning element. 19. The load sharing component of claim 18 wherein the pre-tensioning element and resilient bearing member are arranged such that in use the elastomeric member can be compressed axially a pre-determined distance, until the first clamp member and second clamp member touch. 20. The load sharing component of any preceding claim wherein the resilient bearing member is annular to allow one of an auxiliary line component, a tubular, a riser or a landing string to pass there-through. 21. The load sharing component of any preceding claim, wherein the resilient bearing comprises a ring, whose wall has an undulating profile. 22. A pre-tensioning element for use in the load sharing component of any preceding claim, the pre-tensioning element comprising a pair of annular clamp members housing an elastomeric annular member there-between. 23. A pretensioing element of claim 22, wherein the clamp members each comprise an annular groove for seating the elastomeric member and accommodating deformation of the elastomeric member.
